items.headline: City Council Approves Digital Signage Agreement with Clear Channel
items.summary: The council amended its agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC for large format digital signage on city-owned property. This affects what advertising and messaging residents will see on public spaces throughout San José.

items.headline: City Moving Forward with Terminal Elevator Replacement Project
items.summary: Council took action on the Terminal Elevator Replacement Project, though details on scope, cost, and timeline are not fully specified in the agenda. This likely affects transit infrastructure at a major transportation hub.

items.headline: San José Issues RFP for Encampment Cleanup Services
items.summary: The city is requesting proposals from service providers to handle encampment cleanup operations. This represents the city's formal procurement process for addressing homeless encampments across San José.

items.headline: City negotiating property purchase with school district
items.summary: Cupertino is in closed-door negotiations with the Cupertino Union School District over the purchase of property at 10480 Finch Avenue, with City Manager Tina Kapoor and Interim City Attorney Floy Andrews leading talks on price and payment terms. This could signal a significant land acquisition that may affect future development or school/city cooperative projects in the area.

items.headline: Milpitas adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The city council approved a comprehensive master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District area. This plan will guide new green space and recreational infrastructure development in a key part of Milpitas.

items.headline: City Council updates meeting procedures and protocols handbook
items.summary: The council approved changes to how it conducts meetings and operates. Residents should check what procedural changes may affect public participation or meeting accessibility.

items.headline: Council votes on nine new homes plus accessory units on Old Trace Road
items.summary: The city is considering a proposal to subdivide a vacant 1-acre lot into nine single-family homes and seven junior accessory dwelling units on Old Trace Road. This quasi-judicial hearing will determine whether the residential subdivision is approved.

items.headline: Supermicro invests $2.5M in equipment at Rock Avenue facility
items.summary: Tech company Supermicro is installing new equipment at its 988 Rock Avenue location in a $2.5 million project. This represents significant continued investment in San José's tech manufacturing sector.
